title: anthemis montana subsp. alpina
intro: Anthemis montana subsp. alpina, also known as Alpine Chamomile or Mountain Mayweed, is a subspecies of the Anthemis montana plant, belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and calming effects. The health benefits of Alpine Chamomile include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce anxiety and stress, and promote relaxation, making it a popular herbal remedy for promoting sleep and reducing menstrual cramps. Alpine Chamomile can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or oil, and is also used in herbal creams, salves, and capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
